title: Understand numerators and denominators
topic_path: Module 5: Fractions as numbers on the number line
Topic B: Unit fractions and their relation to the whole

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the numerator of the fraction $\dfrac{1}{2}$ mean?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The $\maroonD{\text{numerator}}$ is the number of equal parts we are counting.

The $\blueE{\text{denominator}}$ is the number of equal parts in one whole. 

$\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}{\blueE{\text{denominator}}}$

The fraction $\dfrac{\maroonD{1}}{\blueE{2}}$ represents cutting a whole into $\blueE{2}$ equal parts and counting $\maroonD{1}$ of those parts. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$=\dfrac{\maroonD{1}}\blueE{2}=\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}\blueE{\text{denominator}}$

The numerator of $\dfrac{\maroonD{1}}{\blueE{2}}$ is $\maroonD{1}$.
The numerator means that we are counting $1$ part.

The fraction $\dfrac{\maroonD{4}}{\blueE{3}}$ represents cutting a whole into $\blueE{3}$ equal parts and counting $\maroonD{4}$ of those parts. 

 

[[☃ image 1]]

$=\dfrac{\maroonD{4}}\blueE{3}=\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}\blueE{\text{denominator}}$

The numerator of $\dfrac{\maroonD{4}}{\blueE{3}}$ is $\maroonD{4}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]
The numerator means that we are counting $4$ parts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the denominator of the fraction $\dfrac32$ mean?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The $\maroonD{\text{numerator}}$ is the number of equal parts we are counting.

The $\blueE{\text{denominator}}$ is the number of equal parts in one whole. 

$\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}{\blueE{\text{denominator}}}$

The fraction $\dfrac{\maroonD{3}}{\blueE{2}}$ represents cutting a whole into $\blueE{2}$ equal parts and counting $\maroonD{3}$ of those parts. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$=\dfrac{\maroonD{3}}\blueE{2}=\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}\blueE{\text{denominator}}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
The $\blueE{2}$ in $\dfrac{\maroonD{3}}\blueE{2}$ is the $\blueE{\text{denominator}}$, so there are $\blueE{2}$ equal parts in one whole.
The denominator means that there are $2$ parts per whole.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the $\maroonD{5}$ represent in ${\dfrac{\maroonD{5}}\blueD{2}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The $\maroonD{\text{numerator}}$ is the number of equal parts we are counting.

The $\blueE{\text{denominator}}$ is the number of equal parts in one whole. 

$\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}{\blueE{\text{denominator}}}$

The fraction $\dfrac{\maroonD{5}}{\blueE{2}}$ represents cutting each whole into $\blueE{2}$ equal parts and counting $\maroonD{5}$ of those parts. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$=\dfrac{\maroonD{5}}\blueE{2}=\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}\blueE{\text{denominator}}$


[[☃ explanation 2]]
The $\maroonD{5}$ in $\dfrac{\maroonD{5}}\blueE{2}$ is the $\maroonD{\text{numerator}}$, so we are counting $\maroonD{5}$ equal parts.
The number $5$ represents the number of parts we are counting.

choices.content: The number of equal parts in one whole
The number of equal parts in all the wholes
The number of parts we are counting
